extraction.one_line_summary: CORT Magnus won the sprint from the break after CAVAGNA Rémi's long solo effort, while ROGLIČ Primož took crucial 6-second bonus by outsprinting CARAPAZ Richard for second.
tactical_patterns: ROGLIČ contested intermediate sprint despite GC lead, revealing Jumbo's concern about small margins with crosswinds threatening time splits.
UAE and Bora controlled peloton instead of Jumbo-Visma, suggesting weak teams burn energy while dominant squads conserve for crosswind splits.
EF didn't work at front all day yet CARTHY stayed well-positioned, showing strong teams can hide riders before crucial stages.
CAVAGNA Rémi from 6-rider break went solo, typical breakaway pattern where strongest rouleur attacks before finale on moderate climbs.

rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: Sprinted from third wheel for 6-second bonus on non-sprint stage, shows calculated bonus hunting when GC margins tight
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: neutral: Recognized danger of ROGLIČ bonus hunting and responded, but lacked sprint speed to counter from second position
rider_signals.CORT Magnus: positive: Won from early breakaway sprint against faster finishers like ROGLIČ, improving climbing allows him to contest mountain transition stages
rider_signals.CAVAGNA Rémi: positive: Strong enough to solo from 6-rider break on hilly stage, confirms rouleur capability on moderate mountain stages
rider_signals.CARTHY Hugh: positive: EF team morale elevated by stage win, positioned well without working, team confidence matters entering final mountain stage
rider_signals.STANNARD Robert: positive: Made consecutive breakaways on back-to-back stages, elite breakaway specialists can sustain repeated efforts
rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: neutral: Previous stage win noted but not relevant to mountain stage tactics
